A.missing: no dispelling-gloom (NOT feel bad) component; generic feel-good via doing
A.reason: Someone feels good and can make another feel good; lacks the gloom-dispelling criterial core and reads more like kindness.

B.missing: the quality itself's own good feel is not asserted; only that seeing X makes others feel good/not bad
B.reason: Captures dispelling gloom (NOT feel bad) via a source others perceive, but omits that the thing itself embodies a bright/good quality.

C.missing: no dispelling-gloom component; generic good-feeling caused by doing
C.reason: Thing feels good and causes another to feel good, but lacks the criterial removal of gloom.

D.missing: no gloom-dispelling; thinking-about-someone reads as fondness not cheer
D.reason: Generic think/feel-good frame fitting many affective concepts, no cheer-specific differentia.

E.missing: no NOT-feel-bad dispelling; proximity-based good feeling generic
E.reason: Being near the thing makes another feel good, but the gloom-dispelling criterion is absent.

F.missing: the quality's own inherent goodness left implicit
F.reason: Seeing the thing makes many people feel good and not feel bad — renders both good-feeling and gloom-dispelling.

G.reason: Thing feels good, others perceive it, and seeing it causes NOT feeling bad — carries both the bright quality and dispelling of gloom.

H.reason: Thing feels good and not bad, others see it and thereby feel good — cleanly renders the cheerful quality plus gloom-dispelling effect.
